events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We need produce raw JSON answer file /app/answer.json. But likely output only JSON? User says response must be raw JSON data without code blocks. Need infer schedule analysis.

We need read schedule: activities, durations, predecessors, early start bars shown. Disruption: Activity J (Insulation) needs 2 extra workdays - rework discovered at handoff. Need report project finish delay in workdays in value field (0 if float absorbs), state whether completion date slips (in rationale maybe), identify what drives answer.

Need compute critical path and total float of J. Let's reconstruct schedule.

From image:
A Mobilize & layout [1d] start 0-1
B Underground rough-in [3d] pred A, start 1-4 (bar from 1 to 4)
C Slab pour [1d] pred B, start 4-5
CC Slab cure [3d] pred C, hatched from 5 to 8
D Framing [6d] pred CC, start 8-14
E Roof dry-in [3d] pred D, start 14-17
F Electrical rough [4d] pred E, start 17-21
G Plumbing top-out [2d] pred E, start 17-19
H HVAC rough [3d] pred E, start 17-20
I Rough inspection [1d] pred F,G,H, start 21-22 (red)
J Insulation [4d] pred I, start 22-26
K Insulation inspection [1d] pred J, start 26-27 (red)
L Drywall hang [2d] pred K, start 27-29
M Tape & finish [3d] pred L, start 29-32
N Paint [4d] pred M, start 32-36
O Trim carpentry [2d] pred N, start 36-38
P Flooring [3d] pred N, start 36-39
Q Final inspection [1d] pred O,P, start 39-40 (red)

So critical path goes through N? Actually O finishes 38, P finishes 39, Q starts 39, finish 40. P has duration 3 and is critical? Let's compute total float for O = 1 day (39-38?). Let's list ES/EF early:
A 0-1
B 1-4
C 4-5
CC 5-8 (lag)
D 8-14
E 14-17
F 17-21
G 17-19
H 17-20
I 21-22
J 22-26
K 26-27
L 27-29
M 29-32
N 32-36
O 36-38
P 36-39
Q 39-40

Project duration 40.

Late times: Q LF=40 LS=39. O LF=39 LS=37, P LF=39 LS=36. N has successors O,P, LF = min(37,36)=36, so N is critical (LS=32). M LF=32, L LF=29, K LF=27, J LF=26, I LF=22, etc. So critical path: A-B-C-CC-D-E-F? Actually I has preds F,G,H; I ES=21 from F (longest). F 17-21 critical. So critical path A-B-C-CC-D-E-F-I-J-K-L-M-N-P-Q. P critical, O has 1 day float. So J is on critical path with zero total float.

Disruption adds 2 workdays to J duration: J becomes 6d, from 22-28. Then K starts 28, L 29, M 32? Wait need recompute:
J ES 22 duration 6 -> EF 28
K ES 28 EF 29
L ES 29 EF 31
M ES 31 EF 34
N ES 34 EF 38
P ES 38 EF 41
Q ES 41 EF 42
O ES 38 EF 40 (still starts after N, finishes 40 before Q start 41? Actually Q pred O,P; O finishes 40, P finishes 41, Q starts 41, EF 42).
Project finish delay 2 workdays (from 40 to 42). Completion date slips by 2 workdays.

Need answer JSON. Also `workable`: what work can still start? Need identify unblocked activities unaffected? The question: "decide what work can proceed, what is blocked, and what recovery plan is valid". It also says "Use `workable` for a list of activity IDs when the task asks what work can still start." Task asks what can proceed. We should list activities that are not blocked by J delay? Since schedule lookahead and disruption affects J onward. Activities before J can proceed as scheduled (or have already occurred). Work can still start for activities with no predecessor constraints to J? A-Q? Let's think. At point of assessment, "actively in progress". J is in-progress and needs 2 extra days. Work blocked: successor of J (K, L, M, N, O, P, Q) are blocked/delayed. Work that can proceed: activities not dependent on J and not already complete? Could be A-I maybe, but they may already be done. Since stage in-progress and J currently, predecessors A-I likely complete/in-progress. The question might ask for workable activities IDs at current lookahead after disruption. Work that can still proceed unaffected: None downstream of J can start on original dates. However A through I are not blocked by
